stations.name: Coorparoo railway station
stations.walk_minutes_note: Closest station for southern Camp Hill streets.

buses.note: Bus routes serve Camp Hill via Old Cleveland Road, Stones Corner, and Cavendish Road. The 200 series buses run frequently along Old Cleveland Road.

driving.motorway_access: M3 corridor for southern travel. Story Bridge for inner CBD via Stanley Street.
driving.approx_cbd_minutes_offpeak_note: Approximately 10 to 14 minutes to Brisbane CBD off-peak.

primary_state.name: Camp Hill State Infants and Primary School

schools.catchment_note: Whites Hill catchment access is one of the central buyer-decision drivers in Camp Hill. The catchment boundary materially affects buyer interest.

lifestyle.shops_dining_note: The Camp Hill Marketplace is the suburb's village strip with cafes, casual dining, and daily-life retail. The Stones Corner precinct sits close by for additional dining and shopping. Coorparoo Square is a short drive for big-format retail and cinema.
lifestyle.topography_note: Camp Hill is named for its elevated terrain. Many streets sit on hills with western or city outlooks, and elevation matters in the pricing conversation.

pockets.name: Whites Hill catchment core
pockets.character: Streets within the Whites Hill State College catchment. Premium pocket for the family-home market.

pockets.name: Camp Hill Marketplace village
pockets.character: Streets close to the Marketplace strip. Walkable to coffee, casual dining, and daily-life retail.

pockets.name: Stones Corner-adjacent
pockets.character: Streets bordering Stones Corner. Different lifestyle access, different price profile.

buyer_profile.note: Parents upgrading from Coorparoo units or smaller East Brisbane properties for the Whites Hill catchment access.
Buyers moving up from Greenslopes, Stones Corner, or Coorparoo who want the family-suburb feel at a more accessible entry point than Bulimba.
Character home stock attracts buyers looking for renovation projects on quieter family streets.
